mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 50% of rate-decline periods.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately trigger negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 65% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when UNRATE remains below 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and builder confidence, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 60-day returns in more than 55% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing output that supports corporate earnings growth, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive returns when FEDFUNDS exceeds 3.5%, or the signal reverses direction during supply-chain constrained periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index surpasses the 240.0 threshold, inflation expectations accelerate and force equity repricing, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 60% of threshold-crossing events, or the signal fails to predict direction when CPIAUCSL growth decelerates.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising total nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rates confirms robust labor market absorption, which sustains consumer spending and produces positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint labor signal predicts negative returns in more than 50% of cases, or the interaction effect vanishes when GDP growth falls below 1.5% quarterly.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly increases in real gross domestic product directly expand corporate revenue bases, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth fails to correlate with 60-day equity returns with Rank IC below 0.01, or positive GDP prints coincide with negative market returns in more than 55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5% signals immediate manufacturing contraction that reduces near-term earnings expectations, causing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the predictive power disappears when the decline is driven by seasonal adjustments rather than underlying demand.
